projects.name: ESP MQTT Controller
projects.description: A lightweight MQTT-based IoT controller for ESP32. Monitor sensors, control relays, and publish data to any MQTT broker with automatic reconnection and secure TLS support.

notes.title: First Time Flashing
notes.content: Make sure your ESP32 is connected via USB. Press and hold the BOOT button, press the EN button once, then release the BOOT button to enter flashing mode.

notes.title: WiFi Configuration
notes.content: After flashing, the device will create a WiFi access point named 'ESP-Config-XXXX'. Connect to it and follow the setup portal to configure your WiFi credentials.

projects.name: ESP BLE Sensor Hub
projects.description: Bluetooth Low Energy sensor hub for ESP32. Reads data from BLE-enabled temperature, humidity, and pressure sensors and forwards them via WiFi or serial output.

notes.title: BLE Compatibility
notes.content: This firmware requires a board with BLE support. ESP32 and ESP32-S3 are fully supported. ESP8266 does not support BLE.

changes: Improved battery life in deep sleep mode
Added support for BME280 environmental sensor

projects.name: STM32 USB HID
projects.description: Custom USB HID firmware for STM32 boards. Transform your STM32 into a programmable keyboard, mouse, or gamepad with configurable button mappings.

notes.title: Bootloader Mode
notes.content: To enter bootloader mode on STM32 boards, set BOOT0 pin to HIGH and BOOT1 pin to LOW, then press the RESET button. Use the browser-based DFU flasher to upload the firmware.

notes.title: Driver Setup
notes.content: Windows users may need to install the Zadig driver tool to replace the default STM32 WinUSB driver for DFU mode. macOS and Linux typically work out of the box.

changes: Fixed HID descriptor parsing for gamepad mode

projects.name: Arduino Blink Pro
projects.description: Advanced blink firmware for Arduino boards with configurable patterns, PWM brightness control, and multiple animation modes accessible via serial commands.
